seoTitle: How to Import a CSV or Excel Spreadsheet to Trello
metaDescription: Convert Excel or CSV spreadsheet rows into Trello cards using an import Power-Up, with column mapping and a safe test-run process.
intro: Migrating a task backlog or client list from a spreadsheet into Trello shouldn't mean copy-pasting row by row. This guide walks through using an import Power-Up to turn your CSV or Excel data into populated cards, with proper column mapping.

steps.heading: Prepare your spreadsheet
steps.body: Clean up your data and save the file as a CSV or Excel document with clear column headers.

steps.heading: Add an import Power-Up
steps.body: On your destination board, click Power-Ups, then Add Power-Ups, and search for an import tool like 'Import to Trello by Blue Cat'.

steps.heading: Authorize the Power-Up
steps.body: Click Authorize to approve the secure data connection between Trello and the import tool.

steps.heading: Upload your file
steps.body: Open the import tool and drag your saved spreadsheet directly into the upload zone.

steps.heading: Map your columns
steps.body: Connect your spreadsheet columns to Trello fields like card name and description on the field-mapping screen.

steps.heading: Choose a destination and import
steps.body: Select which list your new cards should populate, then click 'Check and Import' to run the batch process.

faq.question: Can I import a spreadsheet without a Power-Up?
faq.answer: Trello supports pasting basic text blocks into a list natively, but a dedicated import Power-Up is needed for mapping columns like due dates and labels.

faq.question: What should I do before running a large import?
faq.answer: Run a small test import with a handful of rows first to confirm your column mapping is correct before importing the full file.

faq.question: What columns can I map into Trello cards?
faq.answer: Common mappings include card title, description, due date, and destination list, depending on the import tool you use.

faq.question: What if my import has formatting errors?
faq.answer: Check for corrupted text encoding or missing data cells in your spreadsheet, since these are common causes of import issues.
